title: Erythrophleum cambodianum
intro: Erythrophleum cambodianum, also known as the Cambodian coral tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, with its extracts exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of E. cambodianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used in various herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and topical creams, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Erythrophleum cambodianum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Erythrophleum consists of several species of trees native to tropical Africa and Asia. Erythrophleum cambodianum is specifically found in Cambodia, hence its name. This classification suggests that Erythrophleum cambodianum is closely related to other plants in the pea family, such as beans and legumes.

properties: Erythrophleum cambodianum has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, and it is known to possess various therapeutic properties. Research suggests that the plant contains bioactive compounds with potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The leaves and bark of the plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Erythrophleum cambodianum and its bioactive compounds.

parts: Erythrophleum cambodianum, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its bark and seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing and infection prevention. The bark of the plant is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems, while the seeds are used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. side_effects: Erythrophleum cambodianum is known to have toxic compounds, particularly in its seeds, which can cause side effects if ingested or handled improperly. The plant's seeds contain a compound called erythrophleine, which is highly toxic and can cause serious health issues. If the seeds are ingested, they can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In severe cases, erythrophleine can cause respiratory failure, seizures, and even death. It's essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid ingesting any parts of it.
